async_execution.mechanism: hook_async_execution: async: true on command handlers runs hook in background without blocking; decisions ignored; systemMessage delivered on next turn; asyncRewake: true also wakes Claude on exit code 2

context_injection.mechanism: Hooks return systemMessage field in JSON output to inject context into agent's active session

decision_control.mechanism: block: exit code 2 or decision=block; allow: permissionDecision=allow in hookSpecificOutput; modify: updatedInput replaces tool input before execution; PermissionDenied event supports {retry: true} to allow model retry

handler_types.mechanism: Five types: command (shell), http (POST to URL), mcp_tool (MCP tool call), prompt (LLM evaluation), agent (subagent with tools, experimental); prompt/agent restricted to subset of events

hook_scopes.mechanism: Six scopes: user (~/.claude/settings.json), project (.claude/settings.json), local (.claude/settings.local.json), managed policy, plugin (hooks/hooks.json), component frontmatter (skill/agent)

input_modification.mechanism: hook_input_modification: PreToolUse returns updatedInput in hookSpecificOutput; entire input object replaced; compatible with all permissionDecision values except defer

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Command hooks receive event JSON on stdin; respond with JSON on stdout (exit 0); structured fields: continue, stopReason, suppressOutput, systemMessage, hookSpecificOutput

matcher_patterns.mechanism: hook_matcher_patterns: exact string, pipe-separated list, or JavaScript regex; matches on tool_name or event-specific fields; if field uses permission-rule syntax for per-subcommand filtering

permission_control.mechanism: hook_permission_update_entries: PermissionRequest hooks return updatedPermissions with addRules/replaceRules/removeRules/setMode entries

providerExtensions.name: Hook Event Set
providerExtensions.summary: 29 hook events spanning session, turn, and tool-call cadences: Setup, SessionStart, SessionEnd, InstructionsLoaded, UserPromptSubmit, UserPromptExpansion, PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, PermissionDenied, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, PostToolBatch, Stop, StopFailure, Notification, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, TeammateIdle, ConfigChange, CwdChanged, FileChanged, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove, PreCompact, PostCompact, Elicitation, ElicitationResult. Each event has a defined matcher field; UserPromptSubmit, PostToolBatch, Stop, TeammateIdle, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove, and CwdChanged do not support matchers.

providerExtensions.name: Five Hook Handler Types
providerExtensions.summary: Hooks can be command (shell), http (POST), mcp_tool (call an MCP tool), prompt (LLM eval), or agent (subagent, experimental); prompt/agent restricted to a subset of events.

providerExtensions.name: Hook Scopes and Locations
providerExtensions.summary: Six hook scopes: user, project, local, managed policy, plugin, and component frontmatter; managed policy can lock down the others via allowManagedHooksOnly; plugin hooks force-enabled in managed settings are exempt from allowManagedHooksOnly.

providerExtensions.name: Matcher Pattern Syntax
providerExtensions.summary: Matcher field evaluated as: '*'/empty/omitted = match all; only letters/digits/_/| = exact string or |-separated list; any other chars = JavaScript regex. Per-event fields (tool_name, source, reason, notification_type) drive matching. if field uses permission-rule syntax for subcommand-level filtering (e.g., Bash(git *)).

providerExtensions.name: Decision Control via Exit Codes and JSON Output
providerExtensions.summary: Hooks decide via exit codes (0/2/other) or JSON fields (continue, stopReason, permissionDecision, decision, updatedInput, additionalContext). PermissionDenied event returns {retry: true} to allow model to retry the denied tool call.

providerExtensions.name: Async (Background) Hook Execution
providerExtensions.summary: Command hooks with async: true run in background; decisions ignored, systemMessage delivered on next turn. asyncRewake: true additionally wakes Claude when the background hook exits with code 2, delivering stderr (or stdout if stderr empty) as a system reminder.

providerExtensions.name: PreToolUse Input Modification and Defer
providerExtensions.summary: PreToolUse hooks return updatedInput to replace tool params before execution; defer (CC v2.1.89+) exits Claude for external resume via -p.

providerExtensions.name: Persistent Environment Variable Propagation via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE
providerExtensions.summary: SessionStart/CwdChanged/FileChanged hooks can write export statements to $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE that persist across subsequent Bash commands.

providerExtensions.name: Component-Scoped Hooks in Skills and Agent Frontmatter
providerExtensions.summary: Hooks defined in skill/subagent frontmatter run only while the component is active; skills support a once: true handler flag (honored only in skill frontmatter, not in settings files or agent frontmatter); for subagents, Stop hooks are auto-converted to SubagentStop.

providerExtensions.name: PermissionRequest Permission Update Entries
providerExtensions.summary: PermissionRequest hooks return updatedPermissions with addRules/replaceRules/removeRules/setMode/addDirectories entries scoped to session/local/project/user destinations.

providerExtensions.name: PostToolUse MCP Tool Output Override
providerExtensions.summary: PostToolUse hooks for MCP tools return updatedMCPToolOutput to filter/transform/annotate tool results before Claude sees them.

providerExtensions.name: WorktreeCreate Hook Replaces Default Git Behavior
providerExtensions.summary: WorktreeCreate hook fully replaces Claude Code's git worktree creation, returning the new worktree path; enables non-git VCS support.

providerExtensions.name: Elicitation and ElicitationResult Hooks
providerExtensions.summary: Elicitation/ElicitationResult hooks intercept MCP server input requests, returning accept/decline/cancel actions or modifying the response. Matchers filter by MCP server name.

providerExtensions.name: Conditional Handler Execution via if Field
providerExtensions.summary: Handlers accept an if field using permission-rule syntax to gate execution on conditions. Only evaluated on tool events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, PermissionRequest, PermissionDenied); hooks on other events with an if field never run. For Bash, matched against each subcommand after stripping leading VAR=value assignments.

providerExtensions.name: asyncRewake on Background Command Hooks
providerExtensions.summary: Command hooks with asyncRewake: true wake Claude after completing background execution when exit code is 2, delivering stderr (or stdout if stderr empty) as a system reminder on the next turn. Implies async: true.

providerExtensions.name: disableAllHooks Setting
providerExtensions.summary: Setting disableAllHooks: true in settings.json disables all hook execution for that scope; useful for debugging or locked-down environments.

providerExtensions.name: allowManagedHooksOnly Enterprise Setting
providerExtensions.summary: Enterprise policy setting allowManagedHooksOnly: true restricts hook execution to only managed (admin-deployed) hooks, blocking user and project hooks. Plugin hooks force-enabled in managed settings enabledPlugins are exempt.

providerExtensions.name: Common Handler Fields (timeout, statusMessage, once)
providerExtensions.summary: All hook handler types accept: timeout (seconds before cancel; defaults 600 for command, 30 for prompt, 60 for agent), statusMessage (custom spinner message), and once (runs once per session then removed; honored only in skill frontmatter).

providerExtensions.name: HTTP Hook Fields (headers, allowedEnvVars)
providerExtensions.summary: HTTP hooks accept headers (additional request headers with $VAR or ${VAR} env var interpolation) and allowedEnvVars (list of env var names permitted for interpolation; variables not listed are replaced with empty strings). Non-2xx responses and connection failures are non-blocking errors.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Tool Hook Type
providerExtensions.summary: mcp_tool hooks call a named tool on an already-connected MCP server; accepts server (server name), tool (tool name), and input (arguments with ${path} substitution from hook JSON input). Tool text output treated as command-hook stdout. Server must be already connected; SessionStart/Setup hooks may see 'not connected' on first run.

providerExtensions.name: Per-Command-Hook Shell Selection
providerExtensions.summary: Command hooks accept a shell field ('bash' default or 'powershell') to run the command via PowerShell on Windows. Does not require CLAUDE_CODE_USE_POWERSHELL_TOOL since hooks spawn PowerShell directly.
